title: The Train Sequences
wikitext:
[]: The Train Sequences             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Fri Dec 26, 2008 4:05 pm The thread is an attempt to explain the symbolism of Shinji's train sequences found throughout the series. Hopefully, it'll be more than a series of personal observational notes. They are quite literally "trains of thought," but they always have a set structure and process. The motif is always the same: the insides of a train car, with Shinji sitting on the right side of the car and never on the left side. The left side is reserved for something he's currently confronting in his mind. These sequences always take place when the sun is setting to the west, casting a yellow glow into the cabin. This theme is consistent everytime this scene is shown. If the mythology is taken into account here (as it can, considering Christian, Japanese and Hebrew symbolism is in the show), the Egyptians considered the west as symbolic for death and the east as symbolic for life (as far as the rising and the setting of the sun is concerned). Therefore, Shinji sitting on the right side (East) could easily represent an emerging consciousness and the person or subject across from him could represent something that is dying, or transforming in his consciousness. Rei is often the one who sits on the left, and it is no coincidence that she is helping Shinji transform his consciousness during these scenes. In EoE, we see Shinji on the east side and a younger Shinji on the west side. Applying the concept, Shinji is trying to transform or kill his past memory during Third Impact. Interestingly during this scene, Asuka also appears in his consciousness due to the AT Fields being down, but she appears on the right side, and makes her statement. She is attempting to bring Shinji back to the NOW, back from his analysis from the past. But deeper problems arise, such as Shinji's insecurity of the masks people wear. But in the end, with the three girls and Shinji in the middle of the car, I believe this is a tipping point of Shinji's decision: to stay within his current mindset of the 3 women and let them play out his fantasies (despite the fact they arn't real, which was the cause of his outburst as he figures out), or to play these fantasies in actual reality. It is, potentially, a transformative event, a decision only Shinji can make, hence why everyone is in the middle of the car. Toji's case is a bit more complex. Here, there are actually two streams of consciousness side-by-side, represented here as separate train cars. Shinji is in his usual position, while Rei sits in her position on the left. In the car next to this one, Toji is seen, not sitting down, but standing up and holding onto a hook and looking rather beaten. The fact that he is not sitting down is very significant. It signifies that he is in a position between life and death, which is obvious due to the events that occurred before this moment. Also interesting is the fact that he cannot intrude upon Shinji's consciousness. This is because of the AT Fields that all humans possess around their hearts. The best way to describe these sequences in its entirety is as such: a mode of processing a transformative idea or person within consciousness, while using hidden and subtle symbolism to describe to the viewer what is happening. Consciousness is always moving and evolving. Each "train of thought" is a little different from another.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Dec 26, 2008 4:09 pm It is also worth of note that Rei sitting on the West side symbolizes her death wish (mentioned in EoTV) as well as Lilith physically dying in EoE. Anyway, this is a damn fine post Ironfoot. Give yourself a pat on the back.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Dec 26, 2008 4:12 pm Side note: In episode 4, when Shinji is riding the train, if you are to face the front of the train, Shinji is sitting to the left. Dunno if the dream train sequences has the camera facing the front or the back.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 26, 2008 4:16 pm Just a nitpick, but I have a feeling the metaphor "train of thought" (考えの電車?) doesn't exist in Japanese as such.

[]:             Quote Post by The Goose » Fri Dec 26, 2008 4:39 pm In Rebuild 01, Rei, at one point during the train sequence, is standing in the middle of the train on Shinji's left. Does it mean anything? Is Rei simply being introduced (IE: the concept she is introducing to Shinji) and then taking her place later on the left side of the train (symbolizing a person trying to transform Shinji's consciousness)?

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel² » Fri Dec 26, 2008 4:42 pm I think if we want to introduce the Rebuild Sequences we have to wait for The rebuild to be complete, after all i have a slight feeling that the train symbolism is a bit different in Rebuild. As an essay for the Traditional Evangelion+ EoE i thinks its pretty nice!

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Fri Dec 26, 2008 5:46 pm Ornette wrote: Side note: In episode 4, when Shinji is riding the train, if you are to face the front of the train, Shinji is sitting to the left. Dunno if the dream train sequences has the camera facing the front or the back.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Fri Dec 26, 2008 5:57 pm Well that was quite the good topic post my friend...Now you got me thinking. When Toji was in the next cart, he could still see Shinji but not talk to him..Why could he see him. Is it only when they are asleep that their minds are able to merge to a degree of them being able to see into each others dreams? While they are asleep the ATF still holds strong..I would think that if they were asleep that the ATF's would be easier to slip past for lack of better words. Side question..Is the train going somewhere - I have a few ideas on this - Good post

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Dec 26, 2008 6:02 pm In terms of the location of the train, I've heard it all takes place in Instrumentality, yet Instrumentality itself is supposedly a timeless, spaceless, state. (I believe OMF or one of them mentioned this once.) Personally I think that's very possible. Also note that the scenes of the setting sun and the lighting of the traincar looks as if it's bathed in LCL.....

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel² » Fri Dec 26, 2008 6:11 pm Personally i hope it is indicated to the Instrumentality because it would kinda Prove that a sort of EoE - ish thing will come up in the end. It would be so dissapointing if Rebuild would turn out like a 08/15 ending. P.S. I don`t know if 08/15 is known in English language,but to get sure , it means Ordinary none the less.(i think it comes from the 1st World war or something like that) P.P.S I'm to easy to get caught on Rebuild fanwanking ...

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Dec 27, 2008 2:24 pm In addition, the Leliel sequence implies that this is the same train that young Shinji took out of town when Gendo turned him away, and everything is set as it is due to his memory. The sun was against his back, etc.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Sat Dec 27, 2008 2:53 pm AuraTwilight wrote: In addition, the Leliel sequence implies that this is the same train that young Shinji took out of town when Gendo turned him away, and everything is set as it is due to his memory. The sun was against his back, etc.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Sat Dec 27, 2008 6:06 pm What would have been interesting is if another reality which you know exist in NGE would have been on another track going the opposite way of the children....Just something I thought of. Odd how we don't see Yui on the train talking to him at any point cause I mean she does communicate to him but it seems she can only do this while hes inside 01. hmm..

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Sat Dec 27, 2008 11:12 pm I know that we're trying to stay away from rebuild, but yui/Rei and gendo talking about what to name Shinji seems to take place on a train as well. what could that be about ?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Dec 28, 2008 4:44 am Axell wrote: seems to take place on a train as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Sun Dec 28, 2008 3:30 pm i'm talking about the scene right after the sachi fight in rebuild

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Mon Dec 29, 2008 12:27 am Axell wrote: i'm talking about the scene right after the sachi fight in rebuild

[]:             Quote Post by pat457 » Mon Dec 29, 2008 2:58 am Reichu wrote: Just a nitpick, but I have a feeling the metaphor "train of thought" (考えの電車?) doesn't exist in Japanese as such.
